target.title: fix(discord): require sender context for moderation actions
target.body:
## Summary

- Require trusted Discord requester context before privileged Discord moderation and guild-admin message actions can use requester identity.
- Carry requester identity through CLI, MCP loopback, and gateway message-action paths without relying on client-controlled sender headers.
- Preserve the requester source provider separately from the routeable MCP message channel so cross-source MCP calls cannot borrow Discord sender trust.
- Bind sender-scoped MCP loopback tokens to the current request context, including requester source provider, so requester identity cannot be replayed into another session/channel/source envelope.
- Strip `toolContext.requesterSourceProvider` from non-admin gateway `message.action` callers so requester source trust stays coupled to server-authorized sender identity.
- Honor `requesterSourceProvider` for WhatsApp reaction requester trust so cross-source reactions cannot preserve the wrong sender identity.

## Compatibility / scope notes

- This intentionally fails closed for tool-driven Discord privileged actions from non-Discord requester contexts. Existing cross-channel automation that asks a Discord guild-admin/moderation action to run without a trusted Discord requester or trusted local owner context will now receive an error instead of executing without trusted sender attribution.
- Removed unrelated heartbeat and Gateway WS cosmetic/test hunks from the PR. The `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts` metadata fixture remains because the broad proof suite fails without an explicit manifest metadata snapshot for those PDF model-config assertions.

## Verification

- `git diff --name-only origin/main..HEAD -- '*.ts' '*.mjs' | xargs -r ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts src/channels/plugins/message-actions.security.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.requester-source.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx -` with a redacted runtime proof script importing MCP loopback token/request validation helpers.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Discord privileged moderation and guild-admin message actions now require a trusted Discord requester or trusted local owner context; Gateway strips non-admin spoofed requester sender/source context; MCP loopback sender tokens are bound to the signed channel/session/message/requester-source envelope; WhatsApp reactions only preserve requester identity for same-source WhatsApp requester context.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux/UTC, Node `v22.22.2`, pnpm `11.2.2`, branch `discord-action-sender-guard`, exact PR head `f18dba1d636d1348861a0c0433a8a71565228d06`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node --version
pnpm --version
git rev-parse HEAD
git diff --name-only origin/main..HEAD -- '*.ts' '*.mjs' | xargs -r ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts src/channels/plugins/message-actions.security.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.requester-source.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts
pnpm exec tsx - <<'EOF'
# Redacted runtime proof script executed against OpenClaw runtime modules:
# - setActiveMcpLoopbackRuntime / resolveMcpLoopbackBearerToken
# - validateMcpLoopbackRequest / resolveMcpRequestContext
# Cases: cross-source MCP match, requester-source mismatch, requester-source omission,
# same-source sender token success, and local-owner bearer success.
EOF
```

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the local run on the exact PR head:
```text
$ node --version && pnpm --version && git rev-parse HEAD
v22.22.2
11.2.2
f18dba1d636d1348861a0c0433a8a71565228d06

$ git diff --name-only origin/main..HEAD -- '*.ts' '*.mjs' | xargs -r ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 124ms on 41 files using 1 threads.

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
(exit 0; no diagnostics)

$ git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
(exit 0; no diagnostics)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts src/channels/plugins/message-actions.security.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.requester-source.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts
Test Files 10 passed (10); Tests 244 passed (244) [gateway shard]
Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 22 passed (22) [infra shard]
Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 38 passed (38) [agents shard]
Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 28 passed (28) [discord extension shard]
Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 12 passed (12) [whatsapp extension shard]
Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 3 passed (3) [channels shard]
[test] passed 6 Vitest shards in 79.16s

$ pnpm exec tsx -
[proof] OpenClaw MCP requester-source runtime proof for PR #90481
[proof] git HEAD f18dba1d636d1348861a0c0433a8a71565228d06
[proof] MCP cross-source match: route=discord requesterSource=telegram tokenSource=telegram senderId=<redacted>
[proof] MCP cross-source requester-source mismatch: status=401 body={"error":"unauthorized"}
[proof] MCP cross-source requester-source omitted: status=401 body={"error":"unauthorized"}
[proof] MCP same-source sender match: route=discord requesterSource=<none> senderId=<redacted>
[proof] MCP local-owner bearer success: senderIsOwner=true senderId=<none> requesterSource=telegram
[proof] PASS: MCP requester source is preserved for cross-source sender tokens and fail-closed on mismatch
```

Observed result after fix: Discord privileged action dispatch fails closed before the action handler when requester sender identity is missing. Gateway `message.action` strips spoofed non-admin `requesterSenderId`, `senderIsOwner`, and `toolContext.requesterSourceProvider`, while admin-scoped calls preserve the trusted context. MCP loopback now preserves `requesterSourceProvider` separately from the Discord route provider for cross-source sender tokens, returns 401 when that source is changed or omitted, and still accepts same-source sender tokens and local-owner bearer calls. WhatsApp reaction tests cover the cross-source case where `currentChannelProvider` is WhatsApp but `requesterSourceProvider` is not, and the inverse trusted same-source requester case.

What was not tested: No live Discord guild moderation command was executed against a real Discord bot/guild, and no live external Telegram-to-Discord route was exercised; the runtime proof intentionally stops at the local Gateway/MCP pre-network trust boundary with redacted sender context.
